name: Provision new customers with Trello and Outlook

parameters.text: =✅ Provision new customers with Trello and Outlook — run completed at {{ $now.toFormat('HH:mm') }}

parameters.content:
### Receive Inbound Webhook

Entry point: listens for POST calls on `/wf08697-task`. Use the TEST url while building, switch callers to the PRODUCTION url after publishing.

Next: Normalize Task Payload

### Normalize Task Payload

Custom JavaScript: Normalize incoming task records to a stable schema

Next: Route by Work Type

### Route by Work Type

Routes each item to one of 3 lanes: employee, contractor + fallback.

Ports: ①→ Process 'employee' → Google Sheets · ②→ Fast-Track 'contractor' + Alert · ③→ Hold Unknown Types (DLQ)

### Process 'employee' → Google Sheets

googleSheets — `appendOrUpdate` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Merge Routed Work · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Fast-Track 'contractor' + Alert

microsoftOutlook — `action` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Merge Routed Work · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Microsoft OAuth2

### Hold Unknown Types (DLQ)

googleSheets — `append` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Merge Routed Work · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Merge Routed Work

Rejoins 3 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

Next: Serialize Run Output (CSV)

### Serialize Run Output (CSV)

Serializes items to a CSV binary file.

Next: Archive Row Snapshot

### Archive Row Snapshot

googleSheets — `append` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Fingerprint for Idempotency · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Fingerprint for Idempotency

Custom JavaScript: Build a stable idempotency key per task (djb2 hash, no deps)

Next: Drop Already-Processed Items

### Drop Already-Processed Items

Cross-execution dedupe: remembers keys it has processed before and drops repeats — the idempotency guard.

Next: Stamp Completion Time

### Stamp Completion Time

Stamps `completedAt` with the current time for auditing.

Next: Persist Run Metadata

### Persist Run Metadata

Saves key fields onto the execution record — searchable later in n8n's execution list.

Next: Load Previous Snapshot

### Load Previous Snapshot

googleSheets — `read` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Compute Drift vs Snapshot · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Compute Drift vs Snapshot

Custom JavaScript: Compare current metrics with the previous snapshot

Next: Summarize Run Stats

### Summarize Run Stats

Shapes the item: writes `runAt`, `itemCount`, `status`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

### Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

googleSheets — `append` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Send Summary to Slack · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Send Summary to Slack

slack — `action` operation against the connected account.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Outlook

### Escalate Failure to Outlook

microsoftOutlook — `action` operation against the connected account.

Next: Park in Dead-Letter Queue

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Microsoft OAuth2

### Park in Dead-Letter Queue

googleSheets — `append` operation against the connected account.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

# Provision new customers with Trello and Outlook

**Pattern:** Onboarding Sequence · **Level:** Production · **Trigger:** Webhook

## What it does
Provision new customers with Trello and Outlook. It fires the moment an external system calls its webhook.

## Why it exists
Manual task routing is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Receive Inbound Webhook → Normalize Task Payload → Route by Work Type → Process 'employee' → Google Sheets → Merge Routed Work → Serialize Run Output (CSV) → Archive Row Snapshot → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to onboarding sequence without manual effort
- Anyone automating task routing in software development
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Responds in seconds
- Improves response time
- Keeps your systems in sync

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Google OAuth2 / service account; Microsoft OAuth2; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Failures are also parked in a dead-letter queue for replay. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🔐 Security

Never trust inbound payloads: the webhook path is unguessable. Add header auth or IP allow-listing for defense in depth. Secrets live in n8n credentials — never in node parameters.

## 🧹 Data quality

Dedupe/diff steps make re-runs safe: the same input twice will not create duplicate records. If you change the key fields, clear n8n's dedupe memory (node → 'Clear stored keys') before the next run.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your task routing process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
